reason: All 72 predeclared direction and entry-selectivity configurations had negative development-period mean net returns in favorable, base, and stress costs. No configuration passed the locked development gate, so evaluation and confirmation remained unopened.
strongest_support: Longer lookback reduced trade frequency and LONG 64/2/0.5 improved the base mean by about 3.42 bps per trade versus the same-direction formal default.
strongest_counterevidence: Even the best favorable-cost configuration lost about 12.44 bps per trade. Seventy of 72 base configurations were negative in every development calendar year; the other two were positive only in 2023 and remained negative overall.
interpretation: The first narrowing result points away from tuning entry selectivity alone. Under a 60-minute maximum hold, the event family did not produce enough gross edge to absorb conservative market-order costs. This does not evaluate a longer holding horizon or a different economic mechanism.
